metadata.title: Incorporation of the Fasting Plasma FFA Concentration into QUICKI Improves Its Association with Insulin Sensitivity in Nonobese Individuals
metadata.journal: The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism
metadata.study_design: Euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp study in nonobese subjects with and without family history of type 2 diabetes
metadata.demographics: 57 young, healthy, nonobese individuals (38F/19M), age 19-45 years, white race, BMI <27 kg/m², sedentary lifestyle, no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking. 17 with first-degree relatives affected by type 2 diabetes, 40 without family history of diabetes

evidence.quote: Incorporation of the Fasting Plasma FFA Concentration into QUICKI Improves Its Association with Insulin Sensitivity in Nonobese Individuals
GIANLUCA PERSEGHIN, ANDREA CAUMO, MARIANNA CALONI, GIULIO TESTOLIN, AND LIVIO LUZI
The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism 86(10):4776–4781
we performed a euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp [40 mU/(m2·min)] in 57 young, healthy, nonobese individuals with (n = 17) or without (n = 40) first-degree relatives affected by type 2 diabetes

Fifty-seven healthy, young, nonobese subjects were recruited at the Istituto Scientifico H San Raffaele. The main criteria for their inclusion in the study were the following: 1) age (19–45 yr); 2) white race; 3) body mass index less than 27 kg/m2; 4) sedentary lifestyle; and 5) no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking.
57 young, healthy, nonobese individuals with (n = 17) or without (n = 40) first-degree relatives affected by type 2 diabetes
body mass index less than 27 kg/m2
plasma glucose concentration was kept at 5 mmol/liter for 150 min by means of a variable infusion of 20% dextrose infusion

Plasma insulin was measured with a microparticle enzyme immunoassay technology with no cross-reactions with proinsulin, c-peptide, and glucagon (IMx insulin assay, Abbott Laboratories, Rome, Italy). CV was 7.2 ± 1.7% and 5.7 ± 0.7%, respectively, in the fasting and clamp conditions.

methods_participants.label: Inclusion Criteria - Medical History
methods_participants.value: no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking

evidence.quote: 5) no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking
Subjects were instructed to consume an isocaloric diet and to abstain from exercise activity for 3 wk before the studies.
Plasma was then frozen and stored at −70 C, and FFA determinations were performed as previously described
Body composition, with regional three-compartment analysis (arms, trunk, and legs), was performed with a Lunar Corp.-DPX-IQ scanner

narrative_results.statement: FFA levels during the low-dose insulin clamp were inversely associated with S I(clamp) calculated during both the low- and high-dose insulin clamp

evidence.quote: FFA levels during the low-dose insulin clamp (which is more appropriate to study the antilipolytic insulin action) were inversely associated with the S I(clamp) calculated during both the low- (r (cid:1) -0.57, P (cid:1) 0.033) and high-dose insulin clamp (r (cid:1) - 0.58, P (cid:1) 0.028)

narrative_results.statement: Fasting plasma glucose and FFA concentrations were significantly increased in the offspring of type 2 diabetic parents with respect to normal subjects

evidence.quote: Fasting plasma glucose and FFA concentrations were significantly increased in the offspring of type 2 diabetic parents with respect to the normal subjects

narrative_results.statement: The revised QUICKI found a significant reduction in insulin sensitivity in the offspring group (10%), whereas HOMA-IR and QUICKI did not detect any significant difference

evidence.quote: Whereas HOMA-IR and QUICKI did not detect any significant difference in insulin sensitivity between the two groups, the revised QUICKI found a significant reduction in insulin sensitivity in the offspring group (10%)

narrative_results.statement: The multiple stepwise regression analysis selected log(I b) and log(FFA b) as the best set of predictors of IR

evidence.quote: The multiple stepwise regression analysis selected log(I b) and log(FFA b) as the best set of predictors of IR

semantic_groups.rationale: Identical text and values
Identical text and values
Same fact with minor punctuation difference (hyphen placement in 'Subgroup N' vs 'Subgroup - N')
Same age range (19-45 years) with minor label variation ('Age Range' vs 'Age') and redundant 'years' unit in pass_2
Same BMI criterion (<27 kg/m2) with different phrasing ('BMI' vs 'Body Mass Index', '<27' vs 'less than 27')
Identical text and values
Same weight stability criterion (6-12 months) with minor phrasing differences ('Body Weight Stability' vs 'Weight Stability', added 'stable for at least')
Same insulin infusion rate with minor hyphenation difference in label ('Euglycemic-Hyperinsulinemic' vs 'Euglycemic Hyperinsulinemic')
Hyphen usage differs ('Euglycemic-Hyperinsulinemic' vs 'Euglycemic Hyperinsulinemic') but facts are identical
Hyphen usage differs ('Euglycemic-Hyperinsulinemic' vs 'Euglycemic Hyperinsulinemic') but facts are identical
Different phrasing ('Blood Sampling Interval | 15' vs 'Blood Sampling Frequency | every 15 minutes') but same underlying fact
Pass 2 adds contextual detail ('overnight') but both describe 10-hour fasting period
Pass 1 includes manufacturer details while Pass 2 is more generic, but same measurement value
Pass 1 includes 'technology' suffix in method name but same measurement and value
Identical facts with identical values and negligible wording differences
Identical facts with identical values and negligible wording differences
Same fact with different word order: 'Low Dose Target' vs 'Target (Low Dose)'. Both refer to the same 150 pmol/liter measurement in dose-response clamp study.
Identical extraction: Study Population Total N = 57 in both passes.
Pass 1 has no data while Pass 2 contains inclusion criteria about sedentary lifestyle. This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 has no data while Pass 2 contains inclusion criteria about medical history. This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 has no data while Pass 2 contains high dose insulin infusion parameters (40 mU/(m2·min)). This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 has no data while Pass 2 contains dietary preparation information (isocaloric diet). This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 has no data while Pass 2 contains exercise abstinence period (3 weeks). This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 has no data while Pass 2 contains women study timing information (days 3-8 of menstrual cycle). This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains data on Plasma Glucose Measurement CV
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains data on Plasma Insulin Measurement CV
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains data on FFA Measurement CV
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains FFA Sample Storage Temperature data
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains FFA Assay Timing data
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains Steady-State Period Definition data
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains Steady-State Criteria CV Threshold data
Identical statements about HOMA-IR and SI(clamp) weak association
Identical statements about revised QUICKI improvement
Same fact; pass_2 adds 'group' descriptor to 'offspring' in summary tag
Identical statements about insulin sensitivity reduction percentages
Pass_1 states revised QUICKI found 10% reduction; pass_2 omits this finding and only mentions HOMA-IR and QUICKI found no difference
Identical statements about FFA inverse association with SI(clamp)
Identical statements about regression analysis associations
Same fact; minor wording difference ('predictive association' vs 'best predictors')
Both describe incorporation of fasting FFA into QUICKI improving association with insulin sensitivity. Pass 1 adds 'enhanced its discriminatory power' which is additional detail, not a contradiction.
Both extractions are identical, describing inverse association between SI(clamp), M value, GIR and fasting FFA.

Pass 1 is empty while Pass 2 contains information about revised QUICKI finding significant reduction in insulin sensitivity in offspring group. This is an orphan entry with no corresponding extraction in Pass 1.
